semantic_networks.Semantic Network: Best overall word for connected branching ideas
semantic_networks.Concept Map: Visual diagram of ideas branching from one concept
semantic_networks.Ontology: Formal knowledge system of subjects, categories, and relationships
semantic_networks.Knowledge Graph: Database-style network of entities and relationships
semantic_networks.Topic Map: Organized map of topics and associations
semantic_networks.Lexical Network: Network of words and their relationships
semantic_networks.Semantic Field: Group of words related by meaning

wordplay_patterns.Pangram: Uses every letter of the alphabet at least once (e.g., 'The quick brown fox...')
wordplay_patterns.Anagram: Rearranges letters to make a new word/phrase (e.g., listen -> silent)
wordplay_patterns.Paragram: Changes one or more letters for a pun or joke (e.g., Swan Lake -> Swine Lake)
wordplay_patterns.Lipogram: Writing that deliberately avoids one letter
wordplay_patterns.Heterogram: A word/phrase with no repeated letters
wordplay_patterns.Palindrome: Reads the same forward and backward (e.g., madam, racecar)
wordplay_patterns.Semordnilap: Forms a different word when reversed (e.g., stressed -> desserts)
wordplay_patterns.Ambigram: A word/design readable from more than one angle
wordplay_patterns.Acrostic: First letters of lines spell a message
wordplay_patterns.Portmanteau: Blends parts of words together (e.g., brunch = breakfast + lunch)

ui_design_architecture.Design System: Complete rules and reusable pieces for a product’s UI.
ui_design_architecture.Component Library: Organized set of reusable components for design or code.
ui_design_architecture.Navigation Shell: Main app frame that holds navigation and nested screens.
ui_design_architecture.Responsive Layout: Layout that adapts to phone, tablet, desktop, etc.
ui_design_architecture.State-Aware UI: UI that changes based on Default, Loading, Empty, Error, Success, etc. states.
ui_design_architecture.Design Tokens: Named values for colors, spacing, fonts, shadows, radius, etc.
ui_design_architecture.Hierarchy: Visual importance. Big title first, smaller details second.
ui_design_architecture.Density: How much information is packed into a screen.

ux_principles.User Flow: The path a user takes to complete something.
ux_principles.Information Architecture: How content, pages, tabs, and sections are organized.
ux_principles.Experience Map: A visual map of what the user does, thinks, and feels.
ux_principles.Microinteraction: Small interaction feedback, like button ripples or heart animations.
ux_principles.Accessibility: Designing so people with disabilities can use the app (A11y).
